title: limnophila aquatica
intro: Limnophila aquatica, also known as Marsh Basil or Water Hygrophila, is a type of aquatic plant that belongs to the mint family (Lamiaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Limnophila aquatica include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and act as a natural antioxidant. In herbal preparations, Limnophila aquatica is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
